feedback_text:
DIAGNOSIS: The schema is well-structured with strong real-world features but has syntax issues reducing validity scores and incomplete implementations in pricing models and lifecycle events. Focus on fixing JSON Schema syntax errors first (3 points), then completing hybrid pricing and lifecycle coverage (4.2 points each).

DO NOT CHANGE (these sections are passing):
  ✓ schema_realworld: All production-ready features implemented correctly (8/8 points)
  ✓ schema_completeness.entity_coverage: Strong entity modeling with bonus subscription_changes entity
  ✓ schema_correctness.ref_usage: Good use of $ref patterns and DRY principles
  ✓ schema_correctness.constraint_quality: Comprehensive validation constraints with advanced patterns

REQUIRED FIXES (ordered by point impact):

  FIX 1: schema_completeness.pricing_model_support (current: 75%, 4.2 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Missing full hybrid pricing model implementation
    Expected: Complete hybrid pricing model combining both seat-based and usage-based elements
    Found: Hybrid type exists but conditional validation may be incomplete
    → ACTION: Strengthen the hybrid pricing model by ensuring the conditional schema requires BOTH seat_price AND metered_dimensions when pricing_model.type is 'hybrid', and add validation that hybrid plans must have at least one metered dimension and a valid seat_price > 0.

  FIX 2: schema_completeness.billing_lifecycle (current: 75%, 4.2 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Incomplete billing lifecycle event coverage
    Expected: All lifecycle events modeled: create, upgrade, downgrade, cancel, prorate, trial
    Found: Most events present but some lifecycle transitions may be missing
    → ACTION: Add explicit 'cancel' and 'create' values to the subscription_changes.change_type enum, and ensure the subscription entity includes fields for cancellation_date, cancellation_reason, and creation_source to fully model the create and cancel lifecycle events.

  FIX 3: schema_completeness.entity_coverage (current: 75%, 3.6 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Missing one or more required entities
    Expected: All 6 required entities: tenant, plan, subscription, usage, invoice, line_item
    Found: Most entities present but one may be incomplete or missing
    → ACTION: Verify that the line_item entity is properly defined as a standalone reusable definition (not just embedded in invoices) and ensure the usage_records entity includes all required fields: id, subscription_id, metered_dimension_id, quantity, timestamp, and idempotency_key.

  FIX 4: schema_extensibility.metered_extensibility (current: 75%, 3.2 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Metered dimensions not fully extensible
    Expected: Array of dimension objects allowing unlimited extensibility
    Found: Dynamic array structure exists but may have some hardcoded limitations
    → ACTION: Ensure the metered_dimension definition uses only generic, extensible fields (id, name, unit, aggregation_type) and remove any hardcoded dimension types or constraints that would prevent adding arbitrary new usage dimensions without schema changes.

  FIX 5: schema_correctness.schema_validity (current: 50%, 3.0 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Schema validity check returned 50% instead of 100%
    Expected: Valid JSON Schema draft-07+ syntax throughout
    Found: Valid draft-07 declaration and mostly valid structure, but some syntax issues detected
    → ACTION: Review and fix any JSON Schema syntax errors - check for missing commas, incorrect property nesting, malformed conditional statements (if/then/else), and ensure all $ref paths are correctly formatted and resolvable within the definitions section.

  FIX 6: schema_extensibility.metadata_support (current: 75%, 2.4 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Metadata support not fully open
    Expected: Metadata object on key entities allowing arbitrary extensions
    Found: Advanced metadata structure exists but may have some restrictions
    → ACTION: Ensure the advanced_metadata definition's custom_fields section uses patternProperties with '.*' pattern to allow completely arbitrary field names, and set additionalProperties: true on the main metadata object to allow unlimited extensibility.

  FIX 7: schema_extensibility.versioning (current: 75%, 2.4 pts at stake)
    Failed check: Versioning implementation incomplete
    Expected: Full versioning strategy with migration support
    Found: Version fields and backward compatibility section exist but may lack complete migration strategy
    → ACTION: Add a 'deprecated_fields' array to the backward_compatibility section listing fields scheduled for removal, and include 'migration_scripts' object with version-to-version transformation rules to complete the versioning strategy.
